description_long: This is a rustic, hand-formed clay plant pot with a rounded, bulbous body and a wide, open mouth. The exterior surface is rough and uneven, showing natural variations in color from light tan to deeper brown, with some areas appearing slightly charred or darkened. Around the rim, there are several pronounced, rounded protrusions that give the pot a distinctive, primitive character. The pot's surface is matte and unglazed, with visible textural imperfections and a weathered appearance. Some markings or inscriptions are visible on one side, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used in an archaeological context. The interior is similarly rough and unfinished, consistent with traditional hand-built pottery.
description: A rustic, hand-formed clay plant pot with a wide mouth, rough matte surface, and rounded protrusions around the rim. The pot features earthy tones and visible textural imperfections.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide mouth, rough surface, and several rounded protrusions along the rim.
description_view_1: This angle highlights two prominent rim protrusions and some faint inscriptions on the side.
description_view_2: Side view reveals more inscriptions and a slightly irregular rim with visible indentations.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the bulbous body and the arrangement of rim protrusions, with a clear look at the pot's earthy coloration.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in rustic outdoor gardens or archaeological displays
